english: the house; báṅ-púd (bang phud), first-fruit offering of the barn; rdó-púd (rdo phud), sá-púd (sa phud), an offering of stones or earth, when a house is built, these materials then being used for manufacturing images of gods, Glr.; initiatory present, e.g. the first produce of a work, that has been committed to one Glr. (so, according to circumstances, it may be as much as a specimen); in a general sense, a thing done for the first time; bág-mai (bag ma'i) púd (phud), prob. the first cohabitation. - II. for ṕu-dúṅ (phu dung) and ṕu-dúd (phu dud), q.v.
I. pf. of _o_búd-pa ('bud pa). II. Cs. sbst. 1. spindle covered with yarn. - 2 hair-knot, tuft of hair; púd-càn (phud can), being provided with such a one.

1. adj. perfect, complete, possessing every requisite quality, e.g. dgón-pa (dgon pa), a hermit's dwelling; excellent, exquisite, distinguished, e.g. ro (ro), taste, bsnyén-bkur (bsnyen bkur), distinctions, marks of honour Mil., nor (nor) daṅ (dang) loṅs-spyod (longs spyod) Domaṅ; adv. dge-ba (dge ba) bcu (bcu) pún-sum-tsógs-par (phun sum tshogs par) spyód-pa (spyod pa), Dzl., to practise the ten virtues to perfection. - 2. sbst. perfection, excellence, superior good, frq.; ṕa-ról-poi (pha rol po'i) pún-sum-tsógs-pa-la (phun sum tshogs pa la) cágs-pa (chags pa) to covet the excellent things which another possesses, Thgy. - 3. pún-tsógs (phun tshogs), frequent name for males and females.

1. shield, buckler, Glr., of a convex shape, with the rim bent round; ko-púb (ko phub), a leather buckler; púb-sùbs (phub subs), the cover of a buckler, Cs.; púb-kyi (phub kyi) mé-loṅ (me long), the centre of the shield, Cs. - 2. v. the following.

short straw; púb-ma (phub ma) żig (zhig), a small stalk, a bit of chaff; *púb-ma (phub ma) tàb-ce (tab ce)* or *tab (tab) táṅ-cè (tang ce)*, to fan, to winnow; púb-ldir (phub ldir) Cs., chaff; gró-púb (gro phub), wheat-straw.
1. v. ṕu (phu) - 2. v. _o_púr-ba ('phur ba). - 3. v. púr-pa (phur pa).

peg, pin, nail; rtsíg-pur (rtsig phur) Schr., *púr-ca* (*phur ca*) or *śa** (*sha*) (?) Ld., a peg on a wall, to hang up things; lcàgs-púr (lcags phur), iron nail; śiṅ-púr (shing phur), wooden peg; púr-rnyi (phur rnyi) v. rnyi (rnyi), púr-bżi (phur bzhi) brkyáṅ-ba (brkyang ba) to fasten the hands and feet of a culprit to four pegs driven into the ground, when he is to undergo the punishment of the rkyaṅ-śiṅ (rkyang shing), v. rkyon-ba (rkyon ba). 2. iron instrument in the form of a short dagger, used for expelling evil spirits, and fancied to possess great power, Schl. 257; sá-púr (sa phur) _o_débs-pa ('debs pa), to stick such a dagger into the ground, whereby the subterranean demons are kept off; fig. mig (mig) púr-tsugs-su (phur tsugs su) ltá-ba (lta ba) Glr., to look at one with a piercing glance of the eye; *lha-la (lha la) söl-wa (gsol ba) púr-tsug-tu (phur tsugs tu) _o_déb-pa* ('deb pa) C., to implore a god very earnestly. púr-bu (phur bu) 1. = púr-pa (phur pa); the usual form of incantation is: púr-bus (phur bus) gdáb-bo (gdab bo), tó-bas (tho bas) brdúṅ-ṅo (brdung ngo), pyág-rgyas (phyag rgyas) mnán-no (mnan no)! 2. (gza) (gza') púr-bu (phur bu), the planet Jupiter; its day: Thursday.
Sch.: to emboss; púr-ma (phur ma) or búr-ma ('bur ma), relief work, embossment. - 2. to scratch, v. _o_púr-ba ('phur ba); mgo-púr (mgo phur), n. of a disease Lt.
v. púr-ba (phur ba). - 2. pyé-mai (phye ma'i) púr-ma (phur ma), a decoration resembling a flag.
a medicament Wdn.; púr-tál? (phur thal) S.g.

1. a handful, also púl-gaṅ (phul gang), e.g. of corn, Dzl., beer Lt. (in which case = skyor (skyor)). - 2. end? only in the phrase: púl-tu (phul tu) pyín-pa (phyin pa), to reach the highest degree, to be victorious, to have the better of an argument; yi-gei (yi ge'i) sgrá-la (sgra la) púl-tu (phul tu) pyín (phyin), he has finished his studies in grammar, Glr.; mkás-pai (mkhas pa'i) púl-tu (phul tu) pyín-par (phyin par) gyúr-to (gyur to), he became a great scholar, Pth.; also púl-(tu) (phul tu) byúṅ(-ba) (byung ba), accomplished, perfect, eminent S.g.; p. n. = á-ti-śa (a ti sha). - 3. púl-càn (phul can), thick = *róm-po* (*rom po*) Ld.
1. W. for ṕye (phye); ṕé-ku-líq (phe ku liq), key. - 2. num. figure: 104.
